title: MBTA announces schedule changes, detours as nearly 1 million expected to attend Sunday’s St. Patrick’s Day parade
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: MBTA prepares for nearly 1 million attendees at Sunday's St. Patrick's Day parade in South Boston.
Red Line to operate on a rush hour schedule; shuttle buses will run between South Station and South Boston.
Bus routes 9 and 11 suspended; routes 10, 16, 17, and 47 will face detours on parade day.

title: Boston Public Schools graduation rate reached all-time high last year, still lags behind state average
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Boston Public Schools' graduation rate reached a record 81.3% in 2025, up 1.6% from 2024.
State average graduation rate is 89.3%, eight points higher than Boston Public Schools' rate.
Boston's dropout rate decreased to 3.6%, the lowest on record except for the pandemic-affected 2021.

title: Bruins’ playoff hopes are destined to fail if they can’t solve confounding road woes
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Bruins have a 11-14-5 road record this season, impacting their playoff prospects.
Despite their home success, the Bruins have a seven-game road losing streak, complicating playoff chances.
Boston is currently in the East's second wild-card spot with a 35-22-6 record and 76 points.

title: This Marblehead man is running the Boston Marathon for his pediatric patients
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Brian Wishart, 42, from Marblehead, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for his pediatric patients.
Wishart, Chief of Pediatric Rehabilitation at Spaulding Rehab, aims to support hospital and adaptive sports programs.
He aims to integrate research into clinical care to offer cutting-edge treatments to all children, regardless of resources.

title: 8 children have now died from flu in Mass. this season
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Eight children and 298 adults have died from influenza in Massachusetts this flu season, according to state data.
The most recent pediatric flu fatality occurred between February 15 and February 21, the Department of Public Health reported.
Flu vaccination rates show 38.1% of Massachusetts residents received the vaccine, with officials urging more to get vaccinated.

title: NBA cancels Hawks’ ‘Magic City’ promotion amid backlash following Luke Kornet’s letter
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: The NBA canceled the Atlanta Hawks' Magic City promotion following backlash from a letter by former Celtics player Luke Kornet.
The promotion featured Magic City's chicken wings and a performance by hip-hop artist T.I., set for March 16.
NBA Commissioner Adam Silver cited significant concerns from league stakeholders, including fans, partners, and employees.

title: Shaquille O’Neal had no clue who Celtics guard Baylor Scheierman is
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Shaquille O'Neal was unaware of Celtics guard Baylor Scheierman during a discussion on ESPN's Inside the NBA.
Baylor Scheierman, a first-round pick in 2024, played 59 games this season, averaging 4.8 points and 3.2 rebounds.
Scheierman's playing time hasn't decreased despite Jayson Tatum's return from injury, with both players on a minutes adjustment.

title: Healey calls on aviation company operating at Hanscom to cut ties with ICE
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Gov. Maura Healey urges Signature Aviation to cut ties with ICE over deportation flights at Hanscom Field.
Healey accuses ICE of violating due process and rights, highlighting increased flights under Trump’s second term.
Hanscom Field has seen 132 ICE deportation flights in a year; Healey appeals to airlines and federal officials for change.

title: Delivery person falls through a shaft outside Boston’s Omni Parker House
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: A delivery worker fell 20 feet through a metal grate outside Boston's Omni Parker House, sustaining injuries.
The incident led Boston Inspectional Services to issue a building code violation due to rotted support brackets.
The hotel must inspect all vent grates for safety and submit a repair report following the safety perimeter order.

title: Quincy gas station looking for violist to ‘serenade’ customers
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Quincy Gas owner Harvey Kertzman seeks a violist to serenade customers while they fill their tanks at 451 Quincy Ave.
Signs have been posted for over a year; a retired violist recently accepted the gig starting in warmer weather.
The initiative draws inspiration from old movies featuring musicians serenading diners, similar to European restaurant traditions.
